logic: Dependency checking (CF7 version requirements)
Plugin initialization and action hooking
Filtering hidden form fields to inject version data
Configuration of dynamic field types (text, hidden, email, URL, etc.)
Registration and localization of frontend JavaScript assets
Shortcode registration and handler assignment for various field types (text, submit, label, etc.)
Validation filtering for custom and deprecated form tags
Dynamic attribute configuration and evaluation within the main shortcode handler
Handling of placeholder/value logic based on field options
Enqueuing logic for frontend scripts based on registration status
AJAX request handling for dynamic field evaluation on page load (frontend/backend)
Inclusion of utility, validation, admin, and built-in shortcode files
Initialization and setup of the dtx object.
Iterates over input fields with class 'dtx-pageload' and attribute 'data-dtx-value'.
Parses the 'data-dtx-value' string (e.g., 'CF7_GET key=value att=value').
Handles various shortcode types (CF7_GET, CF7_referrer, CF7_URL, CF7_get_cookie, CF7_guid, CF7_get_current_var, etc.).
For built-in shortcodes (like CF7_GET), it calls the corresponding dtx method to calculate the value.
For shortcodes that don't have a direct server-side equivalent or are queued, it pushes an object to dtx.queue.
If dtx.queue is not empty, it schedules an AJAX call after a 10ms timeout.
The AJAX call sends the queue, the AJAX URL (dtx_obj.ajax_url), and a counter 'n' to the server.
On AJAX success, it iterates through the returned data and updates the corresponding input fields using dtx.set().
dtx.validKey checks if an object has a key that is a non-empty string.
dtx.obfuscate converts the value into HTML entity encoded format if the 'obfuscate' attribute is present.
dtx.set updates the input field's value, adds 'dtx-loaded' class, and triggers the 'dtx_init' event.
dtx.get retrieves a value from the URL query parameters.
dtx.referrer retrieves the document's referrer URL.
dtx.current_url retrieves the full URL or a specified part (scheme, host, port, path, query, fragment).
dtx.get_cookie retrieves a specific cookie value based on the provided key.
dtx.guid generates a random GUID, preferring the browser's crypto API, falling back to Math.random() combined with timestamps/performance.now().
The script waits for the DOM to be ready before calling dtx.init().
Initializes the dtx object and sets up event listeners/initial data fetching.
Iterates over elements with class 'dtx-pageload' and attribute 'data-dtx-value' to queue data retrieval.
Handles various data retrieval methods (CF7_GET, CF7_referrer, CF7_URL, CF7_get_cookie, CF7_guid, etc.) based on the first part of the data-dtx-value.
If data is queued, it triggers an AJAX POST request to retrieve the remaining data.
On successful AJAX response, it updates the corresponding input fields with the retrieved values and adds the 'dtx-ajax-loaded' class.
Uses jQuery's ready function to call dtx.init() upon document load.
The `dtx.current_url` function handles parsing different parts of the URL (scheme, host, port, path, query, fragment).
The `dtx.guid` function generates a unique ID using `window.crypto` if available, otherwise falls back to a combination of timestamp and performance.now().
Initialization/DOM Manipulation (Tag Generator Button Styling)
Tag Generator Dialog Button Styling
String Replacement (replaceAll)
Input Value Encoding and Update (updateOption)
Initial class assignment on buttons within #tag-generator-list targeting elements with data-target containing 'tag-generator-panel-dynamic_'.
Initial class assignment on buttons with data-taggen='close-dialog' within .tag-generator-dialog.
Definition of the 'replaceAll' utility function: Replaces all occurrences of a substring (a) in a string (t) with a replacement string (e). It handles optional global replacement (n).

Definition of the 'updateOption' function: Retrieves the value from the current target element (t.val()). It URL-encodes the value after replacing single quotes with HTML entities ('''). It then updates the value of a sibling hidden input element marked with data-tag-part='option' and triggers a 'change' event on it.
Singleton pattern implementation for TagGenerator class.
Dependency check for Contact Form 7 version 6.0+.
Asset loading (CSS/JS) conditional on admin page hook and dependency.
Initialization of Tag Generators by iterating through `wpcf7dtx_config()`.
Input attribute generation using `default_input_atts` and merging custom/default values.
Logic to handle datalist creation (array) or referencing (string) within the `input` method.
Complex logic within `input` to manage `data-tag-option` and `data-tag-part` based on whether the field is a boolean/radio/checkbox or requires a value.
Conditional rendering of various field types (`text`, `checkbox`, `textarea`) via the `field` method.
Extensive conditional rendering logic within `render` based on the determined `$tag_type` (e.g., 'select', 'checkbox', 'radio', 'textarea', 'number', 'range', 'date', 'label').
Specific logic blocks for rendering specialized fields (e.g., Min/Max/Step for range/number/date, Multiple/Include Blank for select, various options for checkbox/radio).
Final rendering of the footer section containing the output tag input/textarea and the 'Insert Tag' button.
Initialization of shortcodes by hooking into 'init' action.
Shortcode handling for retrieving GET variables (CF7_GET) with sanitization, filtering, and obfuscation options.
Shortcode handling for retrieving POST variables (CF7_POST) with multiple layers of sanitization and filtering.
Shortcode handling for retrieving current URL (CF7_URL), supporting parts (scheme, host, path, query) and protocol filtering.
Shortcode handling for retrieving the HTTP Referrer URL (CF7_referrer) with protocol filtering.
Shortcode handling for retrieving blog information (CF7_bloginfo) based on a key (e.g., 'name', 'timezone').
Shortcode handling for retrieving post fields (CF7_get_post_var) based on key (e.g., 'post_title', 'acf_id') and post ID.
Shortcode handling for retrieving post meta fields (CF7_get_custom_field) with access checks and validation.

Shortcode handling for retrieving current object variables (CF7_get_current_var), dynamically checking if the current object is a User, Post, Term, Archive, or Search/404, and retrieving relevant data.
Shortcode handling for retrieving current user data (CF7_get_current_user) based on a key, only if the user is logged in.
Shortcode handling for retrieving media attachment data (CF7_get_attachment) by ID or by fetching the featured image ID from a post ID, supporting return types ('id' or 'url').
Shortcode handling for retrieving cookie values (CF7_get_cookie) with default value and obfuscation.
Shortcode handling for retrieving taxonomy terms (CF7_get_taxonomy) for a specific post ID, supporting different field types ('names', 'slugs', 'ids').
Shortcode handling for retrieving theme modification options (CF7_get_theme_option) using `get_theme_mod`.
Shortcode handling for generating a GUID (CF7_guid), using `com_create_guid` if available, otherwise generating a standard format GUID.
Protocol filtering and merging (wpcf7dtx_allow_protocols)
Value type detection (wpcf7dtx_detect_value_type)
Sanitization based on type (wpcf7dtx_sanitize)
Escaping based on type/obfuscation (wpcf7dtx_escape)
Post ID retrieval and validation (wpcf7dtx_get_post_id, wpcf7dtx_validate_post_id)
Dynamic value retrieval (wpcf7dtx_get_dynamic)
Dynamic attribute retrieval (wpcf7dtx_get_dynamic_attr, wpcf7dtx_dynamic_attr)
HTML attribute formatting (wpcf7dtx_format_atts)
Field property definition (wpcf7dtx_get_allowed_field_properties)
HTML generation for various fields (wpcf7dtx_input_html, wpcf7dtx_checkbox_html, wpcf7dtx_checkbox_group_html, wpcf7dtx_textarea_html, wpcf7dtx_options_html, wpcf7dtx_select_html)
Array key checking (wpcf7dtx_array_has_key)
Access control checks for post meta/user data (wpcf7dtx_post_meta_key_access_is_allowed, wpcf7dtx_user_data_access_is_allowed)
Settings management (wpcf7dtx_get_settings, wpcf7dtx_update_settings)
String parsing (wpcf7dtx_parse_allowed_keys, wpcf7dtx_split_newlines)
Front-end warning alerts (wpcf7dtx_access_denied_alert)
Adds custom validation messages to Contact Form 7 messages.
Adds 'dtx_disallowed' error code to available CF7 error codes.
Filters validation results for DTX form tags, handling single and multiple selections.
Validates a single form field value based on its type (email, tel, number, date) and length constraints.
Validates DTX form tags within the backend mail configuration, checking for disallowed sensitive data access in default/placeholder values.
Initializes the main validator hook by checking dependencies and adding the validation logic to 'wpcf7_config_validator_validate'.
Validates if a given string content (usually a shortcode) attempts to access sensitive data based on the shortcode name and 'key' attribute.
The main validation flow checks required fields, then type-specific validation (email, tel, number, date), and finally length constraints (min/max).
Initialization of CF7DTX_Plugin_Settings class and execution of constructor.
Registration of settings ('cf7dtx_settings') and sections/fields via `settings_init` hook.
Displaying the settings page under the 'wpcf7' menu using `options_page` hook.
Conditional rendering logic in `render_options_page` based on URL parameters (dismissal, scan execution).
Scan execution logic: If `scan-meta-keys` is in GET, it calls `wpcf7dtx_scan_forms_for_access_keys`.
Form submission logic: If `save-allows` is in POST, it verifies nonce and calls `handle_save_allows`.
Settings rendering logic: `render_options_page` handles displaying the main form, scan results, or notices.
Field rendering logic: `render_field` uses a switch statement to render various input types (text, textarea, select, etc.) based on field configuration.
Scan result rendering logic: `render_scan_results` checks if all detected keys are already allowed and displays appropriate notices/forms.
Save handling logic: `handle_save_allows` iterates through POST data, extracts meta/user keys, merges them with existing settings, and updates the options.

Key extraction logic: `wpcf7dtx_scan_forms_for_access_keys` queries CF7 forms, iterates through tags, and parses shortcode attributes (`CF7_get_custom_field` and `CF7_get_current_user`) to extract keys.
URL generation logic: Helper functions generate nonced URLs for the settings page and the scan screen.
Compares the current plugin version (WPCF7DTX_VERSION) with the stored database version (cf7dtx_version) via `wpcf7dtx_update_check`.
If versions differ, it updates the database version and schedules `wpcf7dtx_update` to run on `admin_init`.
`wpcf7dtx_update` calls `wpcf7dtx_v4_2_0_access_scan_check` to perform version-specific checks.
`wpcf7dtx_v4_2_0_access_scan_check` checks the status option (`cf7dtx_v4_2_0_access_scan_check_status`).
If status is empty, it scans forms using `wpcf7dtx_scan_forms_for_access_keys` (defaulting to 20 forms).
It sets the status to 'intervention_required' if keys are found or if the maximum scan count is reached.
It sets the status to 'intervention_not_required' otherwise.
`wpcf7dtx_access_keys_notice` checks if the status is 'intervention_required' and if the user is an administrator.
It prevents displaying the notice on the settings/scan result screens.
It outputs an admin notice containing links to settings, scanning, and more information.
`wpcf7dtx_set_update_access_scan_check_status` updates the status option in the database.

data_sinks.description: AJAX request handling for dynamic field evaluation on page load (frontend/backend)
Handles various shortcode types (CF7_GET, CF7_referrer, CF7_URL, CF7_get_cookie, CF7_guid, CF7_get_current_var, etc.).
If dtx.queue is not empty, it schedules an AJAX call after a 10ms timeout.
The AJAX call sends the queue, the AJAX URL (dtx_obj.ajax_url), and a counter 'n' to the server.
On AJAX success, it iterates through the returned data and updates the corresponding input fields using dtx.set().
dtx.get_cookie retrieves a specific cookie value based on the provided key.
Handles various data retrieval methods (CF7_GET, CF7_referrer, CF7_URL, CF7_get_cookie, CF7_guid, etc.) based on the first part of the data-dtx-value.
If data is queued, it triggers an AJAX POST request to retrieve the remaining data.
On successful AJAX response, it updates the corresponding input fields with the retrieved values and adds the 'dtx-ajax-loaded' class.
Shortcode handling for retrieving cookie values (CF7_get_cookie) with default value and obfuscation.
Compares the current plugin version (WPCF7DTX_VERSION) with the stored database version (cf7dtx_version) via `wpcf7dtx_update_check`.
If versions differ, it updates the database version and schedules `wpcf7dtx_update` to run on `admin_init`.
`wpcf7dtx_set_update_access_scan_check_status` updates the status option in the database.
